title: Maximilian Veers

{{Quote|I am General ''Maximillian Veers''. ''[''sic'']'' I ''will'' have results.|Maximilian Veers|<ref name="Doctor Aphra 37">[[Doctor Aphra (2016) 37|''Doctor Aphra'' (2016) 37]]</ref>}}
'''Maximilian Veers''' was a [[human]] [[Gender|male]] who served as a [[general]] in the [[Galactic Empire]]'s [[Imperial Army|army]]. In that capacity, he fought in the [[Galactic Civil War]], which pitted the Galactic Empire against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]]. Veers was famous for his leadership during the Imperial [[Battle of Hoth|assault]] on the then Rebellion-occupied [[Hoth]] system, during which he led a squadron of [[All Terrain Armored Transport|AT-AT walkers]] past Rebel defenses and personally destroyed the [[Deflector shield generator|shield generator]] powering [[Echo Base|Rebel base's]] [[deflector shield]]. His leadership in the battle was critical to the successful takeover of the Hoth planetary system, a key victory for the Galactic Empire. Veers himself was critically injured during the battle when a [[Rogue Squadron]] [[snowspeeder]] crashed into his AT-AT's cockpit, destroying the walker, but ultimately survived.

==Biography==
===Early service===
{{Quote|If there's a proper navy someday, young Veers here will be in charge of it, mark my words.|Pell Baylo, whose level of seriousness was unclear|<ref name="Living Force" />}}
A [[human]] [[Gender|male]], Maximilian Veers was [[Reproduction|born]] on the [[planet]] of [[Denon]]<ref name="USW" /> in or after [[44 BBY]].<ref name="Birth date" /> By the age of eleven at the oldest, he had come to serve as a [[Navy|naval]] [[cadet]] of the [[Galactic Republic]] under [[Captain]] [[Pell Baylo]].<ref name="Living Force" /> Later, Veers would serve as an [[Imperial Army|army]] [[officer]] of the [[Galactic Empire]], an authoritarian [[Politics|regime]] that governed most of [[the galaxy]].<ref name="USW" /> While a [[lieutenant]], Veers collaborated with the criminal syndicate [[Crimson Dawn]], meeting the public face of the syndicate, [[Dryden Vos]], aboard Vos' [[First Light|personal yacht]]. Vos made note of the [[officer]]'s intelligence.<ref name="Crimson Climb">''[[Crimson Climb]]''</ref>

===The skilled general===
Several years later, Veers was involved in the Empire's [[Galactic Civil War|struggle]] against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]], which contested the [[Imperial Era|rule]] of [[Galactic Emperor|Emperor]] [[Darth Sidious|Palpatine]].<ref name="ESB" /> Rising in the ranks through cunning and courage, Veers took part in over a dozen battles, including conflicts against the [[Zalorian]]s [[Zaloriis campaign|on]] [[Zaloriis]] and the [[Culroonian]]s [[Culroon III campaign|on]] [[Culroon III]].<ref name="wannabe">''[[The Empire Strikes Back: So You Want to Be a Jedi?]]''</ref> Aside from being a cunning [[army]] [[Imperial officer|officer]], Veers was also dedicated to his [[Maximilian Veers's family|family]].<ref name="Character Encyclopedia" />

[[File:Veers_and_Kosh.png|thumb|left|200px|General Veers worked with Captain Kosh following the Battle of Yavin.]]
Veers was eventually assigned to the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]] [[battle station]], but was ultimately not on board during the [[Battle of Yavin]], where it was destroyed by the Rebel Alliance. The destruction of the Death Star also helped accelerate his career due to the large amount of Imperial officers [[Death|killed]] during the battle.<ref name="Force Collection">{{ForceCollection|General Veers|star=3}}</ref>

Following the Battle of Yavin, Veers already served as a [[general]]. At that time, he was tasked with [[hunting]] down a [[governor]] who betrayed the Empire and locating the former [[senator]] [[Johhar Kessen]]. The Empire believed that Kessen would play a key role in finding the location of the Rebel Alliance's new headquarters, and for the duration of that mission, Veers was assisted by [[Captain]] [[Kosh]], an officer stationed on [[Tatooine]] and in command of the [[68th Legion]].<ref name="SWC">''[[Star Wars: Commander]]''</ref>

===Weeds in a storm===
{{Quote|You're mistaken. They're not vermin. They're weeds. Alas, we are the storm. When the storm blows, weeds bend. When the storm passes, they're unharmed. If the Rebellion were a tree, it'd be different... Until then, they will continue to slip through our fingers|General Veers' assessment of the Rebellion, given to Ozzel and Vader|<ref name="Shu-Torun Lives">{{StoryCite|story=Shu-Torun Lives|book=Star Wars (2015) 50}}</ref>}}
[[File:OzzelVeersMeetingWithVader-ShuTorunLives.png|thumb|220px|right|Veers and Ozzel met Vader in a spot many others had stood before.]]Sometime later, Veers along with [[Admiral]] [[Kendal Ozzel]], travel to the planet [[Mustafar]] to meet with [[Anakin Skywalker|Darth Vader]], the Emperor's dark enforcer, at [[Fortress Vader|his castle]]. Veers stood along side Ozzel behind Vader as the [[Sith Lord]] looked out over the Mustafar landscape. After the admiral stated that the rebels were vermin and asked Vader on how they would eliminate them, Veers spoke up and informed Ozzel that he was mistaken. He stated that the rebels were not vermin but weeds and that the Empire was like a storm.<ref name="Shu-Torun Lives" />

Veers went on to explain that in a storm, weeds are able to bend and survive just as the Rebellion was capable of doing despite the Empire's constant attacks. The general went on to say that if the Rebels were more like a tree, sturdy and rooted in one place, then it would be different. Veers went on to comment that until the situation changed, the Rebels would continue to slip through their fingers. His comment caused Vader to then recall the same statement Princess Leia made to Grand Moff Tarkin. Vader stated that Veers made an interesting point and went on to explain their next course of action against the Rebels.<ref name="Shu-Torun Lives" />

===Searching for the Rebel base===
{{Quote|General, prepare your troops for a surface attack.|Darth Vader, preparing Veers for the Battle of Hoth|audio=PrepareYourTroopsForASurfaceAttack.flac|<ref name="ESB" />}}
[[File:Veers-hd.png|thumb|220x220px|left|Veers speaks with Darth Vader.]]
Shortly thereafter, Darth Vader launched [[Project Swarm]], under which thousands of [[Viper probe droid]]s were dispatched across the galaxy in an effort to discover the location of the new Rebel base. Incensed by the lack of quick progress, Vader ended up executing the project's supervisor, [[Major]] [[Yaltza]]. As a result, General Veers was put in charge of the operation aboard the ''[[Executor]]'', the [[flagship]] of Vader's own [[flotilla]], [[Death Squadron]]. When [[Professor]] [[Rupo Ud|Ud]] pointed out that the Rebels had used at least one ancient site in the past, Veers assembled a team of [[Archaeology|archaeological]] consultants that notably included the rogue scholar [[Chelli Lona Aphra|Chelli Aphra]]. The first lead they investigated was a [[deathshrine]] of the [[Central Isopter]] buried in the [[Kartovian Formation]], on [[Ash Moon 1]]. Veers himself took part in the ensuing expedition. Although the ground crew did not find anything resembling a military base there, Professor Ud was killed after activating a structural trigger, and Aphra's [[Parent|father]] [[Korin Aphra|Korin]] was found lurking on the premises and arrested.<ref name="Doctor Aphra 37" />

Eventually, one of the Viper [[droid]]s detected signs of an [[Echo Base|occupied settlement]] on the sixth planet of [[Hoth]], a [[Hoth system|system]] located in the [[Anoat sector]] of the galactic [[Outer Rim Territories|Outer Rim]]. On the ''Executor'', Veers joined the panel of officers who reviewed the droid's data, along with Vader, Ozzel and his subordinate, Captain [[Firmus Piett]]. While Vader immediately identified the data as proof of the Rebellion, Ozzel was more skeptical. Vader ultimately ordered the fleet to investigate the Hoth [[Star system|system]], and Veers began to prepare for the assault.<ref name="ESB" />

===Victor of Hoth===
{{Quote|Yes, Lord Vader. I've reached the main power generator. The shield will be down in moments. You may start your landing.|Veers, to Vader|<ref name="ESB" />}}
[[File:Armored_Veers_full.png|thumb|150px|right|General Veers fought among his men at Hoth, in full armor.|left]]
Ozzel's approved [[lightspeed]] course, intended to surprise the Rebellion, brought the [[Imperial Navy|Imperial fleet]] out of lightspeed close to the system. As a result, [[Alliance Military|Rebel forces]] on the planet's surface had time to prepare a defense plan and organize an evacuation. Due to a localized energy shield protecting the base and [[power generator]], [[orbital bombardment]] of the base was considered to be an ineffective attack strategy. After informing Vader of such information, Veers was ordered to lead a [[Battle of Hoth|ground assault]] on the power generator and disable the energy shield. Vader then executed Ozzel and promoted Piett to Admiral.<ref name="ESB" />

Veers, who had always kept a low profile in front of Vader,<ref name="SWE" /> had been offered a chance to correct Ozzel's mistake. His [[Blizzard Force|squadron]] of Imperial [[All Terrain Armored Transport|AT-AT walkers]] were deployed beyond the border of the energy shield, allowing a ground approach to be made. Veers led the squadron into the Echo Base [[mountain]] valley, facing heavy Rebel ground fire as he approached the [[Deflector shield generator|shield generators]]. The general led his [[troop]]s from the front, in the [[cockpit]] of the [[Blizzard 1|lead]] [[walker]], as the Rebels prepared to evacuate their headquarters. A group of [[T-47 airspeeder|snowspeeders]] led by Rebel [[Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker]] tried to delay Veers' force, destroying two walkers. As [[Alliance Army|Rebel ground forces]] retreated, Veers ordered his walker to fire on the shield generator, destroying it. The generator's destruction marked the end of the Rebel defense, allowing Vader and [[501st Legion|his men]] to land on Hoth and break into the base, and demonstrating Veers' effectiveness as a military commander.<ref name="ESB" />

During the battle, Blizzard 1 was decapitated when a snowspeeder flown by [[Derek Klivian]] crashed into it.<ref name="OFL">''[[Star Wars: On the Front Lines]]''</ref> Veers almost perished, but was saved by his AT-AT pilot, whose designation was TK-7834. This loyal trooper's name was [[Lastok]]. However, Veers was injured to the point of nearing death, but his unyielding devotion to carrying out Vader's will allowed him to stay conscious and ultimately survive long enough for Imperial medics to assist him.<ref name="FACPOV">{{StoryCite|story=The Truest Duty|book=From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back}}</ref> Veers later infiltrated Echo Base with his [[cold weather assault trooper|snowtrooper]]s.<ref name="USW" /> After the battle, Veers was transported to a medical center near Cardia to undergo treatment for his injuries. After the [[Battle of Exegol]], historian [[Beaumont Kin]] would note in his book ''[[The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]'' that Veers nearly vanished from Imperial records after this point. <ref name="Rise and Fall">''[[Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]''</ref> Veers was also mentioned in the ''[[Galactic Explorer's Guide]]'', a survival guide written by [[Hondo Ohnaka]] and [[DK-RA-43]].<ref name="Galactic Explorer's Guide">''[[Star Wars: The Galactic Explorer's Guide]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Target. Maximum fire power.|Veers, ordering the destruction of Echo Base's shield generator|<ref name="ESB" />}}
[[File:Veers_targets.png|thumb|250px|left|Veers sights his target during the Battle of Hoth.]]
Maximilian Veers was a powerfully built man with [[Color|brown]] [[hair]] and [[eye]]s. Weighing 82 [[kilogram]] and standing 1.93 [[meter]]s,<ref name="TFA head to head" /> he was ten [[centimeter]]s taller than the average [[stormtrooper]]s, the Empire's white-[[armor]]ed shock troops.<ref name="SWE2">{{SWE|groups|stormtroopers|Stormtroopers}}</ref>

Veers was a brilliant tactician,<ref name="OFL" /> and a cool and efficient officer.<ref name="SWE" /> He did not hesitate to put himself in harm's way on the front line, wearing [[Imperial officer battle armor]].<ref name="ESB" /> Cunning and capable, he was also devoted to his family. Because of those qualities, he was viewed as a model Imperial officer.<ref name="Character Encyclopedia" /> An effective and decorated officer, Veers enjoyed the respect of his peers thanks to an impressive record of ground combat,<ref name="TFA head to head" /> and due to Veers' calm demeanor during battle he earned respect from Darth Vader himself.<ref name="OFL" />

Veers always seemed very sure of himself when talking to Vader<ref name="ESB" /> and had learned to tread lightly around the [[Sith]] [[Lord]], though not showing any sign of fear.<ref name="SWE" /> When Veers went to see Vader in [[Darth Vader's meditation chamber|his quarters]], he calmly stood at attention until the meditation chamber opened, and then even tried to defend Ozzel's actions.<ref name="ESB" /> To Veers, serving Vader was an absolute commitment that he was unwilling to fail, believing that his truest duty was to succeed in following the Sith Lord's orders.<ref name="FACPOV" />

==Skills and abilities==
{{Quote|He's very clever, that one. And smart enough not to join the Imperial navy. Ground troops are always easier to manage, and you can do that from relative safety if you want. He's more hands–on, I think, but still. He'll be promoted soon, I'm sure of it.|Dryden Vos, on Veers|<ref name="Crimson Climb" />}}
The [[crime lord]] [[Dryden Vos]] regarded Veers as clever.<ref name="Crimson Climb" /> The dismissive Pell Baylo once made a remark that Veers had a bright future ahead of him in the field of [[Space warfare|space combat]] command should a true [[Republic Navy|Galactic Republic Navy]] arise,<ref name="Living Force" /> with it unclear if what Baylo said was a sarcastic remark or a serious observation of Veers's possible career.<ref name="Living Force Veers Fate">{{Twitter|jjmfaraway|status/1779934183378354179|[[John Jackson Miller]]|quote=That's for the reader to decide, but context certainly makes one option more likely. {{C|In response to: "I have a question about Veer's cameo, if you have a moment. In the book, Baylo remarks "If there's a proper navy someday, young Veers here will be in charge of it, mark my words". Was this him observing Veer's skill or a sarcastic remark to insult Veers." and "we know Veers rises to power in the army instead of the navy. Given Baylo's character, it could be in line for him to insult his cadet as unfit for this specific field, or be in line with him actually observing skill"}}|archivefile=File:Miller-Veersfate.png}}</ref> Instead of being part of the Imperial Navy, Veers would ultimately decide to enter the Imperial Army, where Vos knew officers had an easier time managing affairs. Even then, Veers showed himself to be a more hands on officer than typical army commanders.<ref name="Crimson Climb" /> He also held a degree of authority on the ''Executor'' despite his position as general.<ref name="Doctor Aphra 37" />

==Equipment==
On the battlefield, Veers wore a grey-green [[Imperial officer battle armor|chest armor]] over his duty [[Clothing|uniform]] and a matching open face [[helmet]] with an ample rear skirt and a black chin strap. He was willing to command his operations from the frontline, serving as the [[vehicle commander]] of AT-AT ''Blizzard'' 1 during the Battle of Hoth.<ref name="ESB" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Portrayal===
{{Quote|This man is a general. You don't get to be a general if you're a wimp.|Actor Julian Glover, to George Lucas|<ref name="ITOW">{{InsiderCite|119|In Their Own Words: Making The Empire Strikes Back|In Their Own Words: Making ''The Empire Strikes Back''}}</ref>}}
[[File:Watts.jpg|thumb|250px|Robert Watts as "General Veers."|right]]
General Veers first appeared in the [[1980]] film [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], the second installment in the [[original trilogy]] of ''[[Star Wars]]'', in which he was played by the [[Wikipedia:England|English]] actor [[Julian Glover]].<ref name="ESB" /> [[Robert Watts]], an associate producer on ''The Empire Strikes Back'', invited Glover in, as the two were next-door neighbors at the time.<ref name="ITOW" /> Glover worked on the movie for five days, which he later described as "five days of complete and total bewilderment,"<ref name="WHO">{{InsiderCite|96|The Empire's Finest: Who's Who in the Imperial Military}}</ref> though he admitted to dislike the day he had to shoot inside the AT-AT unable to remember the line "Target the main reactor."<ref>''[[w:c:indianajones:Indymag|Indymag]]'', March [[2015]]</ref>

Despite having a relatively small part in the movie, Glover discussed Veers' relationship to Vader with ''Star Wars'' creator [[George Lucas]]. In Glover's opinion, Veers should not be sycophantic toward Vader, since a proper general would not lack confidence. Lucas approved the actor's vision of the character, allowing Veers to stand up to Vader a little.<ref name="ITOW" />

The armored breastplate worn by Veers in ''The Empire Strikes Back'' was the same design seen on the [[Cold weather assault trooper|snowtroopers]] and the [[bounty hunter]] [[Dengar]].<ref name="Armor">{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/friends-and-enemies-trivia-gallery|text="Friends and Enemies" Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 3 caption}}</ref>

There was potential for Veers to appear in the next [[1983]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. In an early draft of the film's script, Veers was slated to return as the Commander of [[Endor]]'s [[Garrison]], personally leading the [[Battle of Endor|battle]] on the [[moon]].<ref name="MoRotJ">''[[The Making of Return of the Jedi]]''</ref> Robert Watts, co-producer of the movie, contacted Julian Glover to offer him the bit, but the actor was working on something else at the time and could not do it.<ref name="The Villainous Veers">[[Star Wars Insider 190|''Star Wars Insider'' 190]]</ref>

In ''Return of the Jedi'', Robert Watts played [[All Terrain Scout Transport|AT-ST]] pilot Lieutenant [[Blanaid]]. Before this pilot was identified, Watts once said that his character was General Veers. However, that identification was refuted on the official [[StarWars.com]] website, on the grounds that Veers' demotion to a simple pilot was unlikely.<ref name="VeersDB">{{DB|character|generalmaximilianveers|Veers, General Maximilian}}</ref>

The reference book ''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]'' and its [[Ultimate Star Wars, New Edition|newer edition]] confirm that Veers infiltrated Echo Base with snowtroopers,<ref name="USW" /> while the magazine [[Star Wars Helmet Collection 57|''Star Wars Helmet Collection'' 57]] claimed that he died when his AT-AT was destroyed when Klivian crashed his speeder into it.<ref name="HC-57">{{HelmetCollectionCite|57|Helmets}}</ref> The short story "[[The Truest Duty]]" from the anthology ''[[From a Certain Point of View: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' later revealed that Veers was on the verge of death immediately after Klivian's speeder crashed into his AT-AT, but that he had survived.<ref name="FACPOV" />

===Alternate fate===
In the script of ''The Empire Strikes Back'', Veers died when the Rebel snowspeeder [[Rebel pilot|pilot]] Derek Klivian flew his damaged vessel directly into the cockpit of the general's walker. That scene, however, never made it into the final cut of the film,<ref name="VeersDB" /> although it did appear in the [[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|novelization]], written by [[Donald F. Glut]].<ref name="ESBN">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization]]</ref>

Veers' background and fate were expanded upon in a number of reference books and comic books, although those stories are part of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]], formerly known as the Expanded Universe.

On [[October 2]], [[2017]], during an interview with Ed Dolista for the Episode 256 of [[w:c:indianajones:IndyCast|IndyCast]], Julian Glover was asked by Dolista his opinion about Veers' fate. Glover stated that he thinks Veers may have survived since his apparent death happened offstage (although he is aware of Veers' fate in Glut's novelization) and that he could well be brought back if Lucasfilm wants.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://hwcdn.libsyn.com/p/d/9/6/d96715ae3cfde4fe/indycast-021017.mp3|text=c_id=17107724&expiration=1507675868&hwt=d0c3ff8be71d74ccaf207806e8b502f4 IndyCast: Episode 256|work=[https://www.theindycast.com IndyCast]|nobackup=1}}</ref>

===Further appearances===
A young Veers appears in the [[2024]] novel ''[[The Living Force]]'', where he appears as a student under Instructor [[Pell Baylo]],<ref name="Living Force" /> at the suggestion of author [[John Jackson Miller]]. Editor [[Tom Hoeler]] was surprised by the idea but loved the way Miller worked in the cameo.<ref name="Tom Veers">{{Twitter|DarthInternous|status/1777704963403084038|[[Tom Hoeler]]|quote=You might be asking...but how?! I had the same thought when John first made the suggestion. And then I saw it. Perfection. Utter perfection.|archivefile=File:Hoeler-VeersLivingForce.png}}</ref> Additionally, Baylo makes the remark in the novel "If there's a proper navy someday, young Veers here will be in charge of it, mark my words."<ref name="Living Force" /> Canonically, however, Veers is someone who rises to power in the Imperial Army instead of the naval forces.<ref name="ESB" /> When asked if the line was Baylo making an honest observation about Veers's future or instead him making a sarcastic insult that Veers would not go far in the navy, Miller said it was up for fans to decide, yet did note one option was more likely than the other.<ref name="Living Force Veers Fate" />

===Non-canon material===
Maximilian Veers appears as the main antagonist in the 2015 [[LEGO]] animated television series ''[[LEGO Star Wars: Droid Tales]]''. He is the overreaching antagonist in the first three episodes, and the main antagonist in the last two episodes. Following the [[Battle of Endor]], General Veers and his [[Veers' Imperial remnant|remnant group]] of stormtroopers attempted to restart the [[Geonosis droid factories|droid foundries]] on [[Geonosis]] in order to restore the Galactic Empire. His plot involved tricking [[Landonis Balthazar Calrissian|Lando Calrissian]] and his partner [[R2-D2]] into gathering old droids and reusing their parts to build [[battle droid]]s.<ref name="Flight of the Falcon">{{DroidTales|Flight of the Falcon (episode)}}</ref> Veers' scheme was defeated by the combined efforts of R2-D2, Lando, [[C-3PO]], [[Chewbacca]], [[Nien Nunb]], and [[Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar|Ackbar]].<ref name="Gambit on Geonosis">{{DroidTales|Gambit on Geonosis}}</ref>
